title: Top agarose
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"749k4","text":"For use in \"Isolation of cyanophages by plaque assyays\"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"duudf","text":"Prepare 100 mL portions of 0.4 to 0.5% (w/v) of purified agar, agarose or low-melting point (LMP) agarose (i.e., Invitrogen #15517-022) in your media of choice. Although LMP agarose can be quite expensive, it is recommended for temperature sensitive samples and cells, since it solidifies at ca. 25°C. Purified agars, as well as low-melting point agars and agaroses are available for a range of lower temperatures (consult the following websites for more details: www.sigmaaldrich.com and www.invitrogen.com)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":463,"length":21},{"key":1,"offset":489,"length":18}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"8610A1989293432AA952022628D51884","url":"http:// www.sigmaaldrich.com"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"AD7D7DD7BE9640449B34537322916457","url":"http://www.invitrogen.com/"}}}}
steps: Autoclave or microwave sterilize on the day of the assay.
Dispense 2.5 to 3 mL into 13-×-100–mm glass disposable culture tubes (Fisher Scientific #1496127).
Transfer tubes to a water bath or dry heat block set at the appropriate temperature. (e.g., ca. 30 to 32°C for LMP agarose, ca. 40–42°C for purified agar or agarose)
Cover tubes with foil or cap, allow for temperature to equilibrate.

For each water sample, prepare triplicate tubes. Control tubes containing cells are only used to monitor lawn growth. For best results (smooth lump-free top agar), use freshly prepared top agar/agarose since repeated re-melting of solidified agar/agarose can give inferior results.
creator: Amy Chan
